([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [หมู่บ้าน](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99 "wikt:หมู่บ้าน"); [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **mu ban**, pronounced [[mùː bâːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai")) is the lowest [administrative sub-division of Thailand](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Administrative divisions of Thailand"). Usually translated as '[village](/wiki/Village "Village")' and sometimes as '[hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)")', they are a subdivision of a [tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon") (subdistrict). As of 2008[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Muban&action=edit), there were 74,944 administrative mubans in Thailand.[[1]](#cite_note-1) As of the 1990 census, the average village consisted of 144 households or 746 persons. The average land area of villages in Thailand is very small, its average area is about 6.84 km2 (2.64 sq mi), and its average population is also very small, at only 932 people.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Administrative village in Thailand

For the clapper used in Chinese music, see [Paiban](/wiki/Paiban "Paiban").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/79/%E0%B8%97%E0%B8%B5%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%97%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%9C%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%89%E0%B9%83%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%8D%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%88_7_%E0%B8%95.%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%82%E0%B8%9E_%E0%B8%AD._%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%87%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B4%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%88.%E0%B8%9E%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%84%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%A8%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B5%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%98%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:%E0%B8%97%E0%B8%B5%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%97%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%9C%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%89%E0%B9%83%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%8D%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%88_7_%E0%B8%95.%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%82%E0%B8%9E_%E0%B8%AD._%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%87%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B4%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%88.%E0%B8%9E%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%84%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%A8%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B5%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%98%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2_-_panoramio.jpg)

A village chief (*phu yai ban*) office in [tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon") Ban Po, [Bang Pa-in District](/wiki/Bang_Pa-in_District "Bang Pa-in District"), [Ayutthaya Province](/wiki/Ayutthaya_Province "Ayutthaya Province")

| [Administrative divisions of Thailand](/wiki/Organization_of_the_government_of_Thailand "Organization of the government of Thailand") |

| Local divisions |
| | First-level | | --- | | * [Provincial administrative organisation](/wiki/Provincial_administrative_organization "Provincial administrative organization")  (*Ongkan borihan suan changwat*)  ---  * [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok")†  (*Krung Thep Maha Nakhon*) | | Second-level | | * [City municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#City_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban nakhon*)  ---  * [Town municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#Town_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban mueang*)  ---  * [Subdistrict municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#Subdistrict_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban tambon*)  ---  * Subdistrict administrative organisation  (*Ongkan borihan suan tambon*)  ---  * [Pattaya City](/wiki/Pattaya_City "Pattaya City")†  (*Mueang Pattaya*) | | Subdivisions of Bangkok | | * [District](/wiki/List_of_districts_of_Bangkok "List of districts of Bangkok") (*Khet*) * [Subdistrict](/wiki/Khwaeng "Khwaeng") (*Khwaeng*) | | Subdivisions of City municipality | | * *[Khwaeng](/wiki/Khwaeng "Khwaeng")* | |
| Note: † Special administrative area |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Template:Administrative divisions of Thailand") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Template talk:Administrative divisions of Thailand") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative divisions of Thailand") |

**Muban** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [หมู่บ้าน](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99 "wikt:หมู่บ้าน"); [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **mu ban**, pronounced [[mùː bâːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai")) is the lowest [administrative sub-division of Thailand](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Administrative divisions of Thailand"). Usually translated as '[village](/wiki/Village "Village")' and sometimes as '[hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)")', they are a subdivision of a [tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon") (subdistrict). As of 2008[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Muban&action=edit), there were 74,944 administrative mubans in Thailand.[[1]](#cite_note-1) As of the 1990 census, the average village consisted of 144 households or 746 persons. The average land area of villages in Thailand is very small, its average area is about 6.84 km2 (2.64 sq mi), and its average population is also very small, at only 932 people.

## Nomenclature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Muban&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Nomenclature")]

*Muban* may function as one word, in the sense of a hamlet or village, and as such, it may be shortened to *ban*. *Mu ban* may also function as two words, i.e., [หมู่ 'group'](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%AB%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%B9%E0%B9%88 "wikt:หมู่") (of) [บ้าน 'homes'](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99 "wikt:บ้าน").

* *Mu*, in the sense of group (of homes in a tambon), are assigned numbers in the sequence in which each is entered in a register maintained in the district or branch-district office.
* *Ban*, in the sense of [home](/wiki/Home "Home") or [household](/wiki/Household "Household") for members of each group, is assigned a number ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): บ้านเลขที่; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **ban lek thi**) in the sequence in which each is added to the household register also maintained in the district or branch-district office. Each *ban* is registered in the name of a householder ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): เจ้าบ้าน; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **chao ban**). Assigned *ban* and *mu* numbers, together with the names of tambon, district, and province, are used as [geographic addresses](/wiki/Address_(geography) "Address (geography)") by government agencies; [Thailand Post](/wiki/Thailand_Post "Thailand Post") adds a [postal code](/wiki/Postal_code "Postal code"). Village or *ban* names do not usually form part of such official addresses.
* *Ban* in the sense of [Village](/wiki/Village#Southeast_Asia "Village") occurs in [geopolitical](/wiki/Geopolitical "Geopolitical") [toponyms](/wiki/Toponym "Toponym") on maps and [Thai highway network](/wiki/Thai_highway_network "Thai highway network") signage, but these are not administrative subdivisions. Such village names may apply to an isolated *muban*, but typically apply to a group of adjoining ones, which have often been subdivided from the original settlement. Each new *mu* is assigned a new number, in the sequence in which it is registered; existing homes or *ban* in newly numbered *mu* are assigned new numbers starting with one. The village name of the original settlement is usually retained for the larger grouping.

Such village names are not part of a household address, unless *Ban* is retained as part of the toponym when such a settlement is upgraded—e.g., a household in Ban Dan would be addressed as Ban No.\_\_ Mu No.\_\_, Ban Dan Sub-district, [Ban Dan District](/wiki/Ban_Dan_District "Ban Dan District"), Buriram; or #/# T[*ambon*] Ban Dan, A[*mphoe*] Ban Dan, Buriram 31000.

:   Note: Usage of the short form *number/number* for *ban/mu* is both unofficial and unambiguous in a *tambon*, but in city districts it is restricted to the subdivision of an original household registration into additional household registrations.

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Muban&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administration")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B9%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B1%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%9E%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%94%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%94%E0%B8%B5_%28%E0%B8%88%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%AA_%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5%29_%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%87%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B4%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%98%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2_%282025%29_-_img_02.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B9%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B1%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%9E%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%94%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%94%E0%B8%B5_(%E0%B8%88%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%AA_%E0%B8%A3%E0%B8%B1%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A5)_%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%81%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%9A%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%99%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A5%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%87%E0%B8%9B%E0%B8%B0%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B4%E0%B8%99_%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%98%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%B2_(2025)_-_img_02.jpg)

Statue of *Phraya Rattanakun Adunlaya Phakdi*, who in 1892 was elected as a *phu yai ban* in [Bang Pa-in](/wiki/Bang_Pa-in "Bang Pa-in"), making him the first *phu yai ban*. He later became, in 1894, the first *[kamnan](/wiki/Kamnan "Kamnan")* of Thailand as well.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Each such *mu* or group is led by a headman, usually called a village headman or village chief ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): ผู้ใหญ่บ้าน; [RTGS](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"): **phu yai ban**),[[3]](#cite_note-3) who is [elected by the population of the village](/wiki/Elections_in_Thailand#Local_Administration "Elections in Thailand") and then appointed by the [Ministry of the Interior](/wiki/Ministry_of_Interior_(Thailand) "Ministry of Interior (Thailand)"). The headman has two assistants, one for governmental affairs and one for security affairs. There also may be a village committee with elected members from the village, serving as an advisory body for a village. Originally the village headman, once elected, was in office until reaching retirement age. They now only serve for a five-year term but can then apply for reelection. The same is true for the office of [kamnan](/wiki/Kamnan "Kamnan") (กำนัน) or 'sub-district headman' at the next higher tambon (sub-district) level.

Communities ([ชุมชน](https://th.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E0%B8%8A%E0%B8%B8%E0%B8%A1%E0%B8%8A%E0%B8%99 "th:ชุมชน")) or [neighborhoods](/wiki/Neighborhood "Neighborhood") that are part of a town or city (*[thesaban mueang](/wiki/Thesaban_mueang "Thesaban mueang")* and *[thesaban nakhon](/wiki/Thesaban_nakhon "Thesaban nakhon")*) have no equivalent to village headmen, but may be organized into [community associations](/wiki/Community_association "Community association") having advisory committees.

## Other meanings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Muban&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Other meanings")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/Family_houses%2C_Pattaya%2C_Thailand_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-Family_houses%2C_Pattaya%2C_Thailand_-_panoramio.jpg)](/wiki/File:Family_houses,_Pattaya,_Thailand_-_panoramio.jpg)

A gated community (*muban chat san*) in [Pattaya](/wiki/Pattaya "Pattaya"), [Chonburi Province](/wiki/Chonburi_Province "Chonburi Province")

*Muban* (or more fully *muban chat san*, หมู่บ้านจัดสรร), is also the Thai term for '[housing estate](/wiki/Housing_estate "Housing estate")' or '[gated community](/wiki/Gated_community "Gated community")'.
